articles.title: FROM
articles.summary: Pretty much every marketing team on Earth is thinking about ways to improve brand visibility in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and co.

articles.title: 🔎 S [4]tart monitoring your sources for free
FROM ADS TO EPISODES: WHY BRANDS ARE BETTING ON CREATOR‑LED SHOWS
articles.summary: Social-first content is evolving into serialized entertainment, with brands now commissioning full creator-led shows rather than one-off posts. Creator content performs 3-5X better than traditional branded assets when scaled across channels. 63% of 18-34 year-olds stream daily via connected TVs. Brands should act like producers, building operational systems to manage IP, streamline approvals, and repurpose content across platforms.

articles.title: AMAZON REMOVES ALL GOOGLE SHOPPING ADS GLOBALLY IN 48 HOURS
articles.summary: Amazon pulled its ads from Google Shopping across all major markets between July 21 and 23. This included the US, UK, Germany, and Japan, dropping its impression share from up to 60% to zero. This move follows months of reduced ad spend and appears to be a strategic shift aimed at cutting costs, testing paid search value, and asserting greater control over traffic as AI-driven shopping tools gain traction. The withdrawal marks Amazon's most significant advertising exit since its 2020 pullback.

articles.title: 7 WAYS TO GET BETTER RESULTS FROM AI IMAGE GENERATORS
articles.summary: Start by brainstorming ideas and using AI image generators like a sketchbook to explore concepts. Leverage style codes for consistency, prompt AI to help write prompts, and try multiple platforms to find what works best. Focus on signal strength and clarity, not perfection. Use AI to refine your creative process and build reusable workflows with tools like Gemini's Custom Gems for more consistent image generation over time.

articles.title: STEAL THIS UPGRADE EMAIL FROM CALENDLY
articles.summary: Calendly's upgrade email is effective because it spells out the value of paid features. It recommends a specific plan based on user behavior, reducing decision fatigue. It also taps into loss aversion by letting users try premium tools during a free trial. This makes the upgrade feel hard to give up, especially after getting used to the perks.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S INDEX NOW POWERS CHATGPT
articles.summary: ChatGPT appears to use Google's index and cache to retrieve web content, based on tests showing it returns information only from pages submitted to Google. Visibility in ChatGPT depends largely on whether a page is indexed. Issues like “crawled but not indexed” are common and not always critical, but improving internal linking and creating unique content can help important pages get indexed. For better AI visibility, structure content clearly, answer questions directly, use Schema.org markup, and ensure pages work without JavaScript.

articles.title: INTRODUCING THE GOOGLE TRENDS API (ALPHA): A NEW WAY TO ACCESS SEARCH TRENDS DATA
articles.summary: Google launched an alpha version of the Google Trends API. It allows a limited number of developers to access search trends data programmatically. The API provides consistently scaled search interest data over the past 1,800 days, with daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly aggregations. It supports geographic filtering by region and subregion. The API improves on the website version by allowing comparisons across multiple terms and timeframes without re-scaling each request.

articles.title: HOW MCDONALD'S DUG INTO NOSTALGIA AND REIGNITED THE CHICKEN WARS
articles.summary: McDonald's revived its cult-favorite Snack Wrap in July, boosting foot traffic and triggering fresh competition in the chicken category. Popeyes responded with new Chicken Wraps and an AI-generated diss track, while Taco Bell, KFC, Wingstop, and White Castle launched or marketed new chicken items. Chicken remains a key innovation driver in the quick service restaurant space, with brands leaning into nostalgia, cultural campaigns, celebrity tie-ins, and loyalty plays to win market share.

articles.title: HOW HIGH-PERFORMING GTM TEAMS TURN BUYER SIGNALS INTO REVENUE-DRIVING ACTION
articles.summary: GTM teams now use intent data to drive pipeline and speed up sales by focusing on signal strength, not lead volume. Effective programs treat intent as a layer that improves existing channels and combine first-, second-, and third-party data for broader insight. AI now helps teams score signals, predict buyer readiness, and automate follow-up, making intent data more actionable across the entire customer lifecycle.

articles.title: 5 GROWTH LOOPS YOU CAN COPY
articles.summary: Referral, content, and community loops drive more growth than ads alone.

articles.title: WHAT CONTENT STRATEGY LOOKS LIKE IN THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: Publishers are shifting toward newsletters, subscriptions, and events to build direct reader relationships.
